parsed.simulated_user_message: I tried to sketch a quick landscape before bed, but it feels like a luxury I can’t afford right now. I also text a friend, but I’m afraid of adding to their load. I keep drifting between guilt and exhaustion, and sleep still evades me.

parsed.reasoning: I'm here to support you as you navigate these challenging emotions and responsibilities, and it seems like you're trying to find small ways to cope, like sketching and reaching out to a friend. Given your report of trying to sketch and text a friend, I think a strategy of support and facilitation could be helpful, as it acknowledges your efforts and encourages further exploration of your feelings. The evidence of your guilt and exhaustion, as well as your attempts to connect with others, suggests that you're struggling to find a balance between your responsibilities and your own needs. My entry point will be to acknowledge the difficulty of finding time for yourself when everything feels overwhelming, and to explore how we can work together to find small moments of respite.

parsed.final_response: It's great that you tried to sketch before bed, even if it felt like a luxury - that takes a lot of courage when everything feels overwhelming. I can see that you're trying to find small ways to cope, like reaching out to a friend, but the guilt and fear of adding to their load are holding you back. Yeah, it's really tough to find time for yourself when you feel responsible for so many things. Can you think of any other small things you've done in the past to take care of yourself, even if they felt like a luxury at the time?
